description: Standard number panel presets for racing liveries — placement and styling hints used by Shokker Paint Booth to suggest sizes, strokes, and shadows for the Numbers PSD layer.

schema.name: string — human label
schema.description: string — what the preset is for
schema.stroke: { width: px, color: hex } — outline around digits
schema.drop_shadow: { dx, dy, blur, color, opacity } — soft drop shadow

panels.name: NASCAR Door Panel
panels.description: Standard NASCAR-style door number panel — bold 2-3 digit number rendered large enough to read from grandstand cameras.
panels.font_recommendation: Impact, Arial Black, Helvetica Neue Black
tips: Use high contrast against body color
Maintain 8px stroke for TV readability
Center the number in the door cavity, biased slightly forward

panels.name: NASCAR Roof Number
panels.description: Mirror-flipped roof number used by spotters and aerial cameras. Always rotated 90 degrees relative to door.
tips: Orient so spotters in the stands read upright as the car points away from them
No drop shadow — roof contour casts its own shading
Match door panel digit shape exactly

panels.name: GT3 Door Panel
panels.description: FIA GT3 door number — rectangular panel with mandatory white background underlay in some series.
tips: Many GT3 series mandate a contrasting underlay rectangle
Keep 30mm clear-zone around the digits
Class color may be required behind the number

panels.name: GT3 Roundel
panels.description: Circular number roundel popular in IMSA and SRO series — number sits inside a class-colored disc.
tips: Disc fill color encodes class (e.g. yellow for GTD, red for GTP)
Number must be vertically centered — descenders not allowed
Use a 1px white inner ring for added pop

panels.name: F1 Nose Number
panels.description: Modern Formula 1 nose number — small, stylized, often colored to match the team livery.
panels.font_recommendation: Custom — drivers may use personal logos
tips: F1 numbers are stylistic — drivers often use a custom font
Stroke is optional; team livery usually provides contrast
Avoid drop shadows — surface is too small

panels.name: F1 Engine Cover Number
panels.description: Larger number on the engine cover or airbox visible from above and behind for camera identification.
tips: Place high on the engine cover so helicam can read it
Mirror on both sides if asymmetrical livery
Number must fit within FIA-mandated bounding box

panels.name: LMP Class Panel
panels.description: Le Mans Prototype number panel with mandatory class indicator (P1, P2, LMP3, Hypercar). Rectangular with class abbreviation above number.
tips: Class abbreviation sits in a colored bar above the number
Hypercar uses red, LMP2 uses blue, LMP3 uses orange (varies by series)
Panel must be visible from all four cardinal angles

panels.name: IndyCar Sidepod Number
panels.description: Number placed on the sidepod intake area for IndyCar — large, high-contrast, often with team-color outline.
tips: Mirror on both sidepods
Airbox number is mandatory for top-down camera identification
Series logo often sits adjacent — leave breathing room

panels.name: Dirt Late Model Door
panels.description: Massive door number spanning nearly the full door panel, classic Saturday-night-short-track style.
tips: Bigger is better — readability through dust matters
Heavy drop shadow gives depth from grandstand distance
Many drivers use a stylized custom-cut number for brand identity

panels.name: Vintage Roundel
panels.description: Pre-1970s circular number disc — white background, black digits, era-appropriate sans-serif.
tips: Use a slightly off-white (cream) background for period authenticity
Avoid drop shadows — wasn't done back then
Keep the disc thick enough that the number isn't lost in body curvature
